findings.evidence: `rg -n "boundaryFixingSubgroup|mem_boundary" code/TauCeti -g '*.lean'` shows only the abbrev at lines 73-74 and no `mem_boundaryFixingSubgroup_iff`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp] theorem mem_boundaryFixingSubgroup_iff : f ∈ boundaryFixingSubgroup I n ↔ ∀ x ∈ I.boundary M, f x = x`, proved from `mem_fixedOnSubgroup_iff`.
findings.issue: `boundaryFixingSubgroup` has no public membership characterization, so users of the roadmap target `Diff(M, ∂M)` must unfold the abbrev through `fixedOnSubgroup` to prove that an element fixes `I.boundary M` pointwise.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "fixedOnSubgroup_(empty|union|antitone)|mem_fixedOnSubgroup_union" code/TauCeti mathlib/Mathlib -g '*.lean'` finds only Mathlib's generic `fixingSubgroup_empty`, `fixingSubgroup_union`, `fixingSubgroup_antitone`, and `mem_fixingSubgroup_union_iff`, while `Relative.lean` only defines `fixedOnSubgroup_univ` at lines 60-61.
findings.fix: Add named lemmas such as `fixedOnSubgroup_empty`, `fixedOnSubgroup_union`, `mem_fixedOnSubgroup_union_iff`, and `fixedOnSubgroup_antitone` with the appropriate `[simp]` annotation on the empty/univ normal forms.
findings.issue: `fixedOnSubgroup` exposes only membership and `univ`; the characteristic set API promised by the PR and inherited from `fixingSubgroup` is missing for `empty`, `union`, and antitonicity under set inclusion.

summary: The new subgroup names are present, but the public API is not yet characteristic enough for downstream use without unfolding back to the generic fixing subgroup. The boundary-specific main definition also lacks its direct membership/simp lemma.
